How LLMs Work

Visual Guide for Students

Step 1 of 11

What is an LLM?

What is a Large Language Model?

A Large Language Model (LLM) is a computer program that has read billions of sentences and learned patterns in language. It can then predict the next word in a sequence — and by doing this repeatedly, it can write paragraphs, answer questions, and even write code.

Think of it like autocomplete, but much smarter:

"The cat sat on themat"
"Once upon atime"
"The capital of France isParis"

The Big Picture

Text Input
→
Tokenize
→
Embed
→
Transformer Layers
→
Predict Next Word

In the following steps, we will break down each of these stages and show you exactly what happens inside an LLM.
